facts.claim: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 shutdown = true (duplex=half, speed=100, negotiation auto=false)

root_causes.cause: R2 has no route to the 192.168.0.0/24 network, so replies from 172.16.0.12 are not being sent back to R1/192.168.0.12 (they will follow R2's default to R3 instead). This produces an asymmetric/one-way failure: R1->R2 forwards the ping to the destination, but R2 cannot reach the source network to deliver replies.
evidence: Alert: source 192.168.0.12 -> dest 172.16.0.12: 25 pings sent, 0 successful.
R1 routing: static for 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.2 (R1 will forward traffic for 172.16.0.0 to R2).
R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 = 192.168.0.1/24 (source LAN on R1).
R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 = 172.16.0.1/24 (destination LAN on R2).
R2 routing: only 0.0.0.0/0 with fwd 10.0.0.6 (no route for 192.168.0.0/24 present in R2's routing configuration).

data.intent_description: R2 has no route to 192.168.0.0/24 causing asymmetric/one-way failure: pings from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 get no replies. Desired outcome: add route so replies are returned and reachability is restored.

plan.problem: R2 missing route to 192.168.0.0/24; replies from 172.16.0.12 are not forwarded back to 192.168.0.12 (one-way/ asymmetric reachability).
plan.fix_summary: Add a static route on router2 for 192.168.0.0/24 with next-hop toward router1 so return traffic reaches the source LAN.

parameters: next-hop=10.0.0.1
assumption: router1 reachable at 10.0.0.1 on inter-router link; update next-hop if different
operational-risk: if next-hop is unreachable, traffic for 192.168.0.0/24 may be dropped

content.text:
{
  "output": "Type escape sequence to abort.
Sending 5, 100-byte ICMP Echos to 192.168.0.12, timeout is 2 seconds:
Packet sent with a source address of 172.16.0.1 
!!!!!
Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 3/383/1906 ms"
}

recent_changes.summary: Imported running-config for R1: ios_xe_version=16.12; interfaces with IPs: GigabitEthernet0/0/0=192.168.0.1/255.255.255.0, Serial0/1/0=10.0.0.1/255.255.255.252, Serial0/1/1=10.0.0.10/255.255.255.252; GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is shutdown.

Imported running-config for R2: ios_xe_version=16.12; interfaces with IPs: GigabitEthernet0/0/0=172.16.0.1/255.255.255.0, Serial0/1/0=10.0.0.2/255.255.255.252, Serial0/1/1=10.0.0.5/255.255.255.252; GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is shutdown (duplex=half, speed=100, negotiation auto=false).

Imported running-config for R3: ios_xe_version=16.12; interfaces with IPs: GigabitEthernet0/0/0=123.0.0.1/255.255.255.0, Loopback1=203.0.113.1/255.255.255.255, Serial0/1/0=10.0.0.9/255.255.255.252, Serial0/1/1=10.0.0.6/255.255.255.252; GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is shutdown.

facts.claim: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 shutdown = true (duplex=half, speed=100, negotiation auto=false)

evidence: R2 running-config contains a route for 192.168.0.0/24 with next-hop 10.0.0.1 (get_running_config output).
ICMP ping from R2 (source 172.16.0.1) to 192.168.0.12 succeeded 5/5 (100% success; round-trip min/avg/max = 3/383/1906 ms).
R2 Serial0/1/0 (link to R1) is administratively up and operationally up (get_interface_status).
remaining_issues: High ICMP latency/jitter observed (min/avg/max = 3/383/1906 ms) — may warrant further performance investigation.
Serial0/1/0 shows 7 input_errors — investigate physical/link quality if persistent.
Access-list R2_TO_R1 contains an explicit deny for TCP from 172.16.0.0/24 to 192.168.0.0/24 dst-port 443 — this will block HTTPS between those subnets unless intentional.

messages.content:
What was observed:
- One-way failure: 25 pings from 192.168.0.12 -> 172.16.0.12, 0 replies.

Diagnosis (main cause):
- R2 lacked a route to 192.168.0.0/24, so return traffic from 172.16.0.12 did not reach the source (asymmetric routing).

If changes were made:
- Added static route on R2: 192.168.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.1.

Verification:
- Passed.
  - R2 running-config now contains route 192.168.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.1.
  - ICMP from R2 (172.16.0.1) -> 192.168.0.12 succeeded 5/5 (100%).
  - R2 Serial0/1/0 is up.

Remaining issues / follow-ups:
- High ICMP latency/jitter observed (min/avg/max = 3/383/1906 ms) — investigate performance.
- Serial0/1/0 shows 7 input_errors — check physical/link quality.
- ACL on R2 denies TCP from 172.16.0.0/24 to 192.168.0.0/24 dst-port 443 — will block HTTPS unless intentional.

data.last_message:

- One-way failure: 25 pings from 192.168.0.12 -> 172.16.0.12, 0 replies.

Diagnosis (main cause):
- R2 lacked a route to 192.168.0.0/24, so return traffic from 172.16.0.12 did not reach the source (asymmetric routing).

Verification:
- Passed.
  - R2 running-config now contains route 192.168.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.1.
  - ICMP from R2 (172.16.0.1) -> 192.168.0.12 succeeded 5/5 (100%).
  - R2 Serial0/1/0 is up.
